 scripts/phase1e_executor_test.py: syntax ok

 ⏺ All production code is clean. The only remaining asyncio.get_event_loop() in the entire Python tree is the dead branch at scripts/test_round1_unit.py:165 behind if False else asyncio.run(coro) — I'm

 intentionally leaving that alone. It's unreachable at runtime so it can't trigger a deprecation warning, and touching it would be scope creep on a theme fix.

 ⏺ Theme #1 committed as d5a872b . Pushing to keep main.py production-ready — Railway auto-deploys on push, and this is the biggest surface-area change of the session.

 ⏺ Bash (git push origin main 2>&1)

 ⎿   To https://github.com/scadkin/firstcocoagent.git

 210c758..d5a872b main -> main

 ⏺ Pushed. 210c758..d5a872b is live on origin/main — Railway is redeploying right now with the new get_running_loop() call sites throughout handle_message , execute_tool , and all the scheduled scan/check handlers.

 Zero runtime-behavior change expected; only the deprecation warnings go away.

 ctx marker now reads 24% / 26% to EOS (measured — approximately the same as where we started the theme fix since Edit with replace_all=true is a single tool call regardless of match count). Still well below

 the measured 40-percent pause threshold — roughly 16 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) of headroom.

 Next highest-leverage theme candidates from the audit report:

 1. Serper HTTP status unchecked before resp.json() in compliance_gap_scanner , private_schools , fetch_csta_roster , f4_serper_replay , enrich_c4_pass2 . ~5 files, one helper to add or one idiom to propagate. Bug

 shape: failed Serper calls silently produce empty results, scanners quietly return nothing.

 2. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in enrich_c4_pass2 , phase1_ground_truth_lackland , phase2b_sample_fingerprints . ~3 files, wraps past column Z silently. Fix: small helper that handles AA/AB/… column

 naming.

 3. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env with raw Key Error/File Not Found Error . Many files, theme is "add a helpful error at the top". Lower leverage per-fix but widest blast radius.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters across main.py + tools. Case-by-case review needed; less mechanical.

 5. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) that silently no-op after expiry. Needs judgement calls per site.

 My recommendation: Theme #2 = Serper HTTP status check. It's the highest-leverage (failed scanners silently producing empty results is a real-impact data gap, not just a deprecation warning) and the most

 mechanical (add resp.raise_for_status() or equivalent check before every resp.json() on Serper calls). ~5 files, small diff, clear bug shape.

 Alternatively if you want lower-risk work during remaining headroom: Theme #3 (scripts/.env crashes) is the safest — pure error-message polish, zero runtime behavior change.

 What do you want — Serper theme, .env theme, or something else?

 ❯ im ok with whatver you recommend. go

 ⏺ Executing Theme #2 — Serper HTTP status check. Finding all sites first.

 ( asyncio.get_event_loop() deprecation in ~30 sites, unchecked Serper HTTP status in 5+ scripts, .env parse crashes, chr(65+col) column wrap, broad except: pass , hardcoded timestamp windows). One theme-level

 fix is a much higher-leverage use of remaining headroom than picking individual MEDIUMs.

 3. Push everything so far to origin — 7 commits ahead of origin/main . A push is cheap and low-risk before continuing.

 My recommendation: (3) push now (cheap insurance), then (2a) theme fix #1 = asyncio.get_event_loop() → asyncio.get_running_loop() across main.py + research_engine.py . That's the highest-leverage theme and

 aligns with what we've been touching today. HIGH-5 can bundle with the next GAS-only session.

 ctx marker now reads 24% / 26% to EOS (measured — approximately the same as where we started the theme fix since Edit with replace_all=true is a single tool call regardless of match count). Still well below

 the measured 40-percent pause threshold — roughly 16 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) of headroom.

 Next highest-leverage theme candidates from the audit report:

 1. Serper HTTP status unchecked before resp.json() in compliance_gap_scanner , private_schools , fetch_csta_roster , f4_serper_replay , enrich_c4_pass2 . ~5 files, one helper to add or one idiom to propagate. Bug

 shape: failed Serper calls silently produce empty results, scanners quietly return nothing.

 2. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in enrich_c4_pass2 , phase1_ground_truth_lackland , phase2b_sample_fingerprints . ~3 files, wraps past column Z silently. Fix: small helper that handles AA/AB/… column

 naming.

 3. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env with raw Key Error/File Not Found Error . Many files, theme is "add a helpful error at the top". Lower leverage per-fix but widest blast radius.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters across main.py + tools. Case-by-case review needed; less mechanical.

 5. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) that silently no-op after expiry. Needs judgement calls per site.

 My recommendation: Theme #2 = Serper HTTP status check. It's the highest-leverage (failed scanners silently producing empty results is a real-impact data gap, not just a deprecation warning) and the most

 mechanical (add resp.raise_for_status() or equivalent check before every resp.json() on Serper calls). ~5 files, small diff, clear bug shape.

 Alternatively if you want lower-risk work during remaining headroom: Theme #3 (scripts/.env crashes) is the safest — pure error-message polish, zero runtime behavior change.

 What do you want — Serper theme, .env theme, or something else?

 ❯ im ok with whatver you recommend. go

 ctx marker now reads 24% / 26% to EOS (measured — approximately the same as where we started the theme fix since Edit with replace_all=true is a single tool call regardless of match count). Still well below

 the measured 40-percent pause threshold — roughly 16 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) of headroom.

 Next highest-leverage theme candidates from the audit report:

 1. Serper HTTP status unchecked before resp.json() in compliance_gap_scanner , private_schools , fetch_csta_roster , f4_serper_replay , enrich_c4_pass2 . ~5 files, one helper to add or one idiom to propagate. Bug

 shape: failed Serper calls silently produce empty results, scanners quietly return nothing.

 2. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in enrich_c4_pass2 , phase1_ground_truth_lackland , phase2b_sample_fingerprints . ~3 files, wraps past column Z silently. Fix: small helper that handles AA/AB/… column

 naming.

 3. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env with raw Key Error/File Not Found Error . Many files, theme is "add a helpful error at the top". Lower leverage per-fix but widest blast radius.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters across main.py + tools. Case-by-case review needed; less mechanical.

 5. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) that silently no-op after expiry. Needs judgement calls per site.

 My recommendation: Theme #2 = Serper HTTP status check. It's the highest-leverage (failed scanners silently producing empty results is a real-impact data gap, not just a deprecation warning) and the most

 mechanical (add resp.raise_for_status() or equivalent check before every resp.json() on Serper calls). ~5 files, small diff, clear bug shape.

 Alternatively if you want lower-risk work during remaining headroom: Theme #3 (scripts/.env crashes) is the safest — pure error-message polish, zero runtime behavior change.

 What do you want — Serper theme, .env theme, or something else?

 ❯ im ok with whatver you recommend. go

 ⏺ Executing Theme #2 — Serper HTTP status check. Finding all sites first.

 - tools/private_schools.py:170 : 1 site .

 - tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py:146 : 1 site .

 Already-protected sites (for context): research_engine.py (1, via raise_for_status ), district_prospector.py (3, via status-check guards), lead_importer.py (1), 4 script files (7 total), call_processor.py (1),

 socal_filter_pass4.py (1) = 14 already-protected sites (measured).

 ⏺ Pushed — 7 commits, c696481..210c758 , main → origin/main. S70 HIGH fixes are durable now.

 Step 2 — asyncio theme fix

 Grep scope first: the audit said ~30 call sites measured in main.py + 5 in research_engine.py — let me get exact counts before editing.

 ⏺ Counted: 100 sites in agent/main.py (measured, counted from grep), 7 sites in tools/research_engine.py (measured), 1 site in scripts/phase1e_executor_test.py (measured), 1 dead-branch site in

 scripts/test_round1_unit.py (measured, behind if False else ). The audit's "~30 measured" estimate was a significant undercount — actual is ~109 live sites. Still mechanical.

 Before bulk-replacing, verifying that the research_engine.py sites (the only non-main.py production sites) are all inside async def contexts — get_running_loop() raises RuntimeError if called outside one.

 ⏺ Verified — all functions containing the call sites are async def (lines 222, 226, 319, 533, 1173, 1514, 4000-4608 from the grep). Every site is inside an async context. Safe to do the bulk replace.

 ⏺ Pushed. 210c758..d5a872b is live on origin/main — Railway is redeploying right now with the new get_running_loop() call sites throughout handle_message , execute_tool , and all the scheduled scan/check handlers.

 Zero runtime-behavior change expected; only the deprecation warnings go away.

 ctx marker now reads 24% / 26% to EOS (measured — approximately the same as where we started the theme fix since Edit with replace_all=true is a single tool call regardless of match count). Still well below

 the measured 40-percent pause threshold — roughly 16 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) of headroom.

 Next highest-leverage theme candidates from the audit report:

 1. Serper HTTP status unchecked before resp.json() in compliance_gap_scanner , private_schools , fetch_csta_roster , f4_serper_replay , enrich_c4_pass2 . ~5 files, one helper to add or one idiom to propagate. Bug

 shape: failed Serper calls silently produce empty results, scanners quietly return nothing.

 2. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in enrich_c4_pass2 , phase1_ground_truth_lackland , phase2b_sample_fingerprints . ~3 files, wraps past column Z silently. Fix: small helper that handles AA/AB/… column

 naming.

 3. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env with raw Key Error/File Not Found Error . Many files, theme is "add a helpful error at the top". Lower leverage per-fix but widest blast radius.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters across main.py + tools. Case-by-case review needed; less mechanical.

 5. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) that silently no-op after expiry. Needs judgement calls per site.

 My recommendation: Theme #2 = Serper HTTP status check. It's the highest-leverage (failed scanners silently producing empty results is a real-impact data gap, not just a deprecation warning) and the most

 mechanical (add resp.raise_for_status() or equivalent check before every resp.json() on Serper calls). ~5 files, small diff, clear bug shape.

 Alternatively if you want lower-risk work during remaining headroom: Theme #3 (scripts/.env crashes) is the safest — pure error-message polish, zero runtime behavior change.

 What do you want — Serper theme, .env theme, or something else?

 ❯ im ok with whatver you recommend. go

 ⏺ Executing Theme #2 — Serper HTTP status check. Finding all sites first.

 - tools/signal_processor.py : 13 sites (lines 1333, 2275, 2504, 2730, 2938, 3324, 3474, 3738, 4077, 4285, 4474, 4665, 4854). All the scan_* and search_* functions bypass status checks.

 - tools/private_schools.py:170 : 1 site .

 - tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py:146 : 1 site .

 Already-protected sites (for context): research_engine.py (1, via raise_for_status ), district_prospector.py (3, via status-check guards), lead_importer.py (1), 4 script files (7 total), call_processor.py (1),

 socal_filter_pass4.py (1) = 14 already-protected sites (measured).

 Total Serper call sites across the codebase: 29 (measured). Fix target: 15 unprotected.

 Applying fixes. For signal_processor.py the 13 sites come in two indent levels (12-space inside function body, 16-space inside for q in queries loop) — two replace_all edits.

 ⏺ Pushed. 210c758..d5a872b is live on origin/main — Railway is redeploying right now with the new get_running_loop() call sites throughout handle_message , execute_tool , and all the scheduled scan/check handlers.

 Zero runtime-behavior change expected; only the deprecation warnings go away.

 ctx marker now reads 24% / 26% to EOS (measured — approximately the same as where we started the theme fix since Edit with replace_all=true is a single tool call regardless of match count). Still well below

 the measured 40-percent pause threshold — roughly 16 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) of headroom.

 Next highest-leverage theme candidates from the audit report:

 1. Serper HTTP status unchecked before resp.json() in compliance_gap_scanner , private_schools , fetch_csta_roster , f4_serper_replay , enrich_c4_pass2 . ~5 files, one helper to add or one idiom to propagate. Bug

 shape: failed Serper calls silently produce empty results, scanners quietly return nothing.

 2. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in enrich_c4_pass2 , phase1_ground_truth_lackland , phase2b_sample_fingerprints . ~3 files, wraps past column Z silently. Fix: small helper that handles AA/AB/… column

 naming.

 3. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env with raw Key Error/File Not Found Error . Many files, theme is "add a helpful error at the top". Lower leverage per-fix but widest blast radius.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters across main.py + tools. Case-by-case review needed; less mechanical.

 5. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) that silently no-op after expiry. Needs judgement calls per site.

 My recommendation: Theme #2 = Serper HTTP status check. It's the highest-leverage (failed scanners silently producing empty results is a real-impact data gap, not just a deprecation warning) and the most

 mechanical (add resp.raise_for_status() or equivalent check before every resp.json() on Serper calls). ~5 files, small diff, clear bug shape.

 Alternatively if you want lower-risk work during remaining headroom: Theme #3 (scripts/.env crashes) is the safest — pure error-message polish, zero runtime behavior change.

 What do you want — Serper theme, .env theme, or something else?

 ❯ im ok with whatver you recommend. go

 ⏺ Executing Theme #2 — Serper HTTP status check. Finding all sites first.

 - tools/signal_processor.py : 13 sites (lines 1333, 2275, 2504, 2730, 2938, 3324, 3474, 3738, 4077, 4285, 4474, 4665, 4854). All the scan_* and search_* functions bypass status checks.

 - tools/private_schools.py:170 : 1 site .

 - tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py:146 : 1 site .

 Already-protected sites (for context): research_engine.py (1, via raise_for_status ), district_prospector.py (3, via status-check guards), lead_importer.py (1), 4 script files (7 total), call_processor.py (1),

 socal_filter_pass4.py (1) = 14 already-protected sites (measured).

 Total Serper call sites across the codebase: 29 (measured). Fix target: 15 unprotected.

 Applying fixes. For signal_processor.py the 13 sites come in two indent levels (12-space inside function body, 16-space inside for q in queries loop) — two replace_all edits.

 10 commits, all pushed, Railway auto-redeployed. ctx marker reads 26% / 24% to EOS (measured) — still below the measured 40-percent pause threshold.

 Next theme options from the audit (priority order):

 1. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic — 3 files ( enrich_c4_pass2.py , phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py , phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py ). Wraps past column Z silently. Small mechanical fix.

 2. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — many files, pure error-message polish.

 3. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — needs judgement calls per site, less mechanical.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass — case-by-case review, highest cognitive load, lowest automation.

 My recommendation: Theme #3 = chr(65+col) column arithmetic. It's the highest-leverage remaining mechanical theme, the 3 scripts are one-shot audit helpers so blast radius is contained, and the fix is a tiny
 shared helper (converts 0-indexed column to A/B/…/Z/AA/AB/… letter). After that, ctx budget permitting, Theme #4 (.env missing) is pure polish.

 redirect.

 ctx marker now reads 24% / 26% to EOS (measured — approximately the same as where we started the theme fix since Edit with replace_all=true is a single tool call regardless of match count). Still well below

 the measured 40-percent pause threshold — roughly 16 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) of headroom.

 Next highest-leverage theme candidates from the audit report:

 1. Serper HTTP status unchecked before resp.json() in compliance_gap_scanner , private_schools , fetch_csta_roster , f4_serper_replay , enrich_c4_pass2 . ~5 files, one helper to add or one idiom to propagate. Bug

 shape: failed Serper calls silently produce empty results, scanners quietly return nothing.

 2. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in enrich_c4_pass2 , phase1_ground_truth_lackland , phase2b_sample_fingerprints . ~3 files, wraps past column Z silently. Fix: small helper that handles AA/AB/… column

 naming.

 3. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env with raw Key Error/File Not Found Error . Many files, theme is "add a helpful error at the top". Lower leverage per-fix but widest blast radius.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters across main.py + tools. Case-by-case review needed; less mechanical.

 5. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) that silently no-op after expiry. Needs judgement calls per site.

 My recommendation: Theme #2 = Serper HTTP status check. It's the highest-leverage (failed scanners silently producing empty results is a real-impact data gap, not just a deprecation warning) and the most

 mechanical (add resp.raise_for_status() or equivalent check before every resp.json() on Serper calls). ~5 files, small diff, clear bug shape.

 Alternatively if you want lower-risk work during remaining headroom: Theme #3 (scripts/.env crashes) is the safest — pure error-message polish, zero runtime behavior change.

 What do you want — Serper theme, .env theme, or something else?

 ❯ im ok with whatver you recommend. go

 - tools/private_schools.py:170 : 1 site .

 Already-protected sites (for context): research_engine.py (1, via raise_for_status ), district_prospector.py (3, via status-check guards), lead_importer.py (1), 4 script files (7 total), call_processor.py (1),

 10 commits, all pushed, Railway auto-redeployed. ctx marker reads 26% / 24% to EOS (measured) — still below the measured 40-percent pause threshold.

 Next theme options from the audit (priority order):

 1. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic — 3 files ( enrich_c4_pass2.py , phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py , phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py ). Wraps past column Z silently. Small mechanical fix.

 2. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — many files, pure error-message polish.

 3. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — needs judgement calls per site, less mechanical.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass — case-by-case review, highest cognitive load, lowest automation.

 My recommendation: Theme #3 = chr(65+col) column arithmetic. It's the highest-leverage remaining mechanical theme, the 3 scripts are one-shot audit helpers so blast radius is contained, and the fix is a tiny

 shared helper (converts 0-indexed column to A/B/…/Z/AA/AB/… letter). After that, ctx budget permitting, Theme #4 (.env missing) is pure polish.

 ※ recap: Draining the S70 overnight audit HIGHs and themes; 10 commits pushed to main, currently queued on Theme #3 (chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in 3 scripts). Next action: start Theme #3 unless you

 redirect.

 under Z — but fragile to future column additions.

 - scripts/phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py:62-64 — local col_letter() helper. Used in a debug print at line 73. Not load-bearing but fixing for consistency.

 10 commits, all pushed, Railway auto-redeployed. ctx marker reads 26% / 24% to EOS (measured) — still below the measured 40-percent pause threshold.

 Next theme options from the audit (priority order):

 1. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic — 3 files ( enrich_c4_pass2.py , phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py , phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py ). Wraps past column Z silently. Small mechanical fix.

 2. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — many files, pure error-message polish.

 3. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — needs judgement calls per site, less mechanical.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass — case-by-case review, highest cognitive load, lowest automation.

 My recommendation: Theme #3 = chr(65+col) column arithmetic. It's the highest-leverage remaining mechanical theme, the 3 scripts are one-shot audit helpers so blast radius is contained, and the fix is a tiny

 shared helper (converts 0-indexed column to A/B/…/Z/AA/AB/… letter). After that, ctx budget permitting, Theme #4 (.env missing) is pure polish.

 ※ recap: Draining the S70 overnight audit HIGHs and themes; 10 commits pushed to main, currently queued on Theme #3 (chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in 3 scripts). Next action: start Theme #3 unless you

 redirect.

 ❯ lets go theme 3

 ⏺ Theme #3 — chr(65+col) fix. Let me find the sites and check if a correct helper already exists in the codebase so we can reuse instead of re-inventing.

 ⏺ Theme #3 plan

 Buggy sites (measured):

 - scripts/enrich_c4_pass2.py — 4 sheet-write sites at lines 972, 980, 1009, 1020. Load-bearing (writes wrong cells if column index ≥ 26). Latent today because state_col=0 , parent_col=6 , notes_col=18 — all

 under Z — but fragile to future column additions.

 - scripts/phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py:62-64 — local col_letter() helper. Used in a debug print at line 73. Not load-bearing but fixing for consistency.

 - scripts/phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py:48 — debug print inside range(20) loop. Cannot manifest today (20 < 26) but fixing for consistency with the audit callout.

 Already-correct helpers:

 - tools/signal_processor.py:258-266 — canonical _col_letter with proper base-26 loop. ✅

 - tools/district_prospector.py:2813-2817 — _col_letter_dp handles A-Z + AA-AZ (up to col 51). Partial but good enough for its own use. Not touching.

 Fix: add a local _col_letter(idx) helper to each of the 3 scripts (same shape as signal_processor._col_letter ) and replace the bare chr(65 + col) / chr(ord("A") + idx) calls. Not importing across module

 boundaries — these are one-shot audit scripts and a 6-line helper is simpler than a cross-module dependency. Out of scope: scripts/phase1d_local_sentinel.py:86 (not flagged by the audit, debug print).

 116 )
 119 + def _col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :
 120 + """Convert 0-indexed column number to A1 letter (A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...).
 121 +
 122 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(65 + col)` which wraps past Z

 123 + silently — chr ( 65 + 26 ) = '[' , breaking any write to a column at index ≥ 26.

 124 + """

 125 + if idx < 0 :

 126 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 127 + result = ""

 128 + while True :

 129 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 130 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 131 + if idx < 0 :

 132 + break

 133 + return result

 10 commits, all pushed, Railway auto-redeployed. ctx marker reads 26% / 24% to EOS (measured) — still below the measured 40-percent pause threshold.

 Next theme options from the audit (priority order):

 1. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic — 3 files ( enrich_c4_pass2.py , phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py , phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py ). Wraps past column Z silently. Small mechanical fix.

 2. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — many files, pure error-message polish.

 3. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — needs judgement calls per site, less mechanical.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass — case-by-case review, highest cognitive load, lowest automation.

 My recommendation: Theme #3 = chr(65+col) column arithmetic. It's the highest-leverage remaining mechanical theme, the 3 scripts are one-shot audit helpers so blast radius is contained, and the fix is a tiny

 shared helper (converts 0-indexed column to A/B/…/Z/AA/AB/… letter). After that, ctx budget permitting, Theme #4 (.env missing) is pure polish.

 ※ recap: Draining the S70 overnight audit HIGHs and themes; 10 commits pushed to main, currently queued on Theme #3 (chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in 3 scripts). Next action: start Theme #3 unless you

 redirect.

 ❯ lets go theme 3

 ⏺ Theme #3 — chr(65+col) fix. Let me find the sites and check if a correct helper already exists in the codebase so we can reuse instead of re-inventing.

 ⏺ Theme #3 plan

 Buggy sites (measured):

 - scripts/enrich_c4_pass2.py — 4 sheet-write sites at lines 972, 980, 1009, 1020. Load-bearing (writes wrong cells if column index ≥ 26). Latent today because state_col=0 , parent_col=6 , notes_col=18 — all

 under Z — but fragile to future column additions.

 - scripts/phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py:62-64 — local col_letter() helper. Used in a debug print at line 73. Not load-bearing but fixing for consistency.

 - scripts/phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py:48 — debug print inside range(20) loop. Cannot manifest today (20 < 26) but fixing for consistency with the audit callout.

 Already-correct helpers:

 - tools/signal_processor.py:258-266 — canonical _col_letter with proper base-26 loop. ✅

 - tools/district_prospector.py:2813-2817 — _col_letter_dp handles A-Z + AA-AZ (up to col 51). Partial but good enough for its own use. Not touching.

 Fix: add a local _col_letter(idx) helper to each of the 3 scripts (same shape as signal_processor._col_letter ) and replace the bare chr(65 + col) / chr(ord("A") + idx) calls. Not importing across module

 boundaries — these are one-shot audit scripts and a 6-line helper is simpler than a cross-module dependency. Out of scope: scripts/phase1d_local_sentinel.py:86 (not flagged by the audit, debug print).

 116 )

 119 + def _col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 120 + """Convert 0-indexed column number to A1 letter (A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...).

 121 +

 122 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(65 + col)` which wraps past Z

 123 + silently — chr ( 65 + 26 ) = '[' , breaking any write to a column at index ≥ 26.

 124 + """

 125 + if idx < 0 :

 126 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 127 + result = ""

 128 + while True :

 129 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 130 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 131 + if idx < 0 :

 132 + break

 133 + return result

 134 +

 135 +

 138 if not email or "@" not in email:

 62 def col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 63 - # 0-indexed → A, B, C...

 64 - return chr(ord("A") + idx)

 63 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 65 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): previous code was `chr(ord("A") + idx)` which

 66 + wraps past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Fixed to handle overflow.

 67 + """

 68 + if idx < 0 :

 69 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 70 + result = ""

 71 + while True :

 72 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 73 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 74 + if idx < 0 :

 75 + break

 76 + return result

 78 +

 79 def dump_row ( abs_row_num: int , row: list ):

 80 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 81 print ( f"\n━━━ Row {abs_row_num} (len= { len (row)} ) ━━━" )

 29 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.
 30 +
 31 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(ord('A') + i)` which wraps

 32 + past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Currently only called with i in

 33 + range ( 20 ) so the wrap cannot manifest, but this is a defensive

 34 + upgrade that matches the fix in enrich_c4_pass2.py and

 35 + phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py.

 36 + """

 37 + if idx < 0 :

 38 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 39 + result = ""

 40 + while True :

 41 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 42 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 43 + if idx < 0 :

 44 + break

 45 + return result

 46 +

 47 +

 48 def filled_tup ( row ):

 49 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 50 return tuple (i for i, v in enumerate (padded[: 20 ]) if str (v).strip() != "" )

 10 commits, all pushed, Railway auto-redeployed. ctx marker reads 26% / 24% to EOS (measured) — still below the measured 40-percent pause threshold.

 Next theme options from the audit (priority order):

 1. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic — 3 files ( enrich_c4_pass2.py , phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py , phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py ). Wraps past column Z silently. Small mechanical fix.

 2. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — many files, pure error-message polish.

 3. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — needs judgement calls per site, less mechanical.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass — case-by-case review, highest cognitive load, lowest automation.

 My recommendation: Theme #3 = chr(65+col) column arithmetic. It's the highest-leverage remaining mechanical theme, the 3 scripts are one-shot audit helpers so blast radius is contained, and the fix is a tiny

 shared helper (converts 0-indexed column to A/B/…/Z/AA/AB/… letter). After that, ctx budget permitting, Theme #4 (.env missing) is pure polish.

 ※ recap: Draining the S70 overnight audit HIGHs and themes; 10 commits pushed to main, currently queued on Theme #3 (chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in 3 scripts). Next action: start Theme #3 unless you

 redirect.

 ❯ lets go theme 3

 ⏺ Theme #3 — chr(65+col) fix. Let me find the sites and check if a correct helper already exists in the codebase so we can reuse instead of re-inventing.

 ⏺ Theme #3 plan

 Buggy sites (measured):

 - scripts/enrich_c4_pass2.py — 4 sheet-write sites at lines 972, 980, 1009, 1020. Load-bearing (writes wrong cells if column index ≥ 26). Latent today because state_col=0 , parent_col=6 , notes_col=18 — all

 under Z — but fragile to future column additions.

 - scripts/phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py:62-64 — local col_letter() helper. Used in a debug print at line 73. Not load-bearing but fixing for consistency.

 - scripts/phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py:48 — debug print inside range(20) loop. Cannot manifest today (20 < 26) but fixing for consistency with the audit callout.

 Already-correct helpers:

 - tools/signal_processor.py:258-266 — canonical _col_letter with proper base-26 loop. ✅

 - tools/district_prospector.py:2813-2817 — _col_letter_dp handles A-Z + AA-AZ (up to col 51). Partial but good enough for its own use. Not touching.

 Fix: add a local _col_letter(idx) helper to each of the 3 scripts (same shape as signal_processor._col_letter ) and replace the bare chr(65 + col) / chr(ord("A") + idx) calls. Not importing across module

 boundaries — these are one-shot audit scripts and a 6-line helper is simpler than a cross-module dependency. Out of scope: scripts/phase1d_local_sentinel.py:86 (not flagged by the audit, debug print).

 62 def col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 63 - # 0-indexed → A, B, C...

 63 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 65 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): previous code was `chr(ord("A") + idx)` which

 66 + wraps past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Fixed to handle overflow.

 67 + """

 68 + if idx < 0 :

 69 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 70 + result = ""

 71 + while True :

 72 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 73 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 74 + if idx < 0 :

 75 + break

 76 + return result

 10 commits, all pushed, Railway auto-redeployed. ctx marker reads 26% / 24% to EOS (measured) — still below the measured 40-percent pause threshold.

 Next theme options from the audit (priority order):

 1. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic — 3 files ( enrich_c4_pass2.py , phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py , phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py ). Wraps past column Z silently. Small mechanical fix.

 2. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — many files, pure error-message polish.

 3. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — needs judgement calls per site, less mechanical.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass — case-by-case review, highest cognitive load, lowest automation.

 My recommendation: Theme #3 = chr(65+col) column arithmetic. It's the highest-leverage remaining mechanical theme, the 3 scripts are one-shot audit helpers so blast radius is contained, and the fix is a tiny

 shared helper (converts 0-indexed column to A/B/…/Z/AA/AB/… letter). After that, ctx budget permitting, Theme #4 (.env missing) is pure polish.

 ※ recap: Draining the S70 overnight audit HIGHs and themes; 10 commits pushed to main, currently queued on Theme #3 (chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in 3 scripts). Next action: start Theme #3 unless you

 redirect.

 ❯ lets go theme 3

 ⏺ Theme #3 — chr(65+col) fix. Let me find the sites and check if a correct helper already exists in the codebase so we can reuse instead of re-inventing.

 ⏺ Theme #3 plan

 Buggy sites (measured):

 - scripts/enrich_c4_pass2.py — 4 sheet-write sites at lines 972, 980, 1009, 1020. Load-bearing (writes wrong cells if column index ≥ 26). Latent today because state_col=0 , parent_col=6 , notes_col=18 — all

 under Z — but fragile to future column additions.

 - scripts/phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py:62-64 — local col_letter() helper. Used in a debug print at line 73. Not load-bearing but fixing for consistency.

 - scripts/phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py:48 — debug print inside range(20) loop. Cannot manifest today (20 < 26) but fixing for consistency with the audit callout.

 Already-correct helpers:

 - tools/signal_processor.py:258-266 — canonical _col_letter with proper base-26 loop. ✅

 - tools/district_prospector.py:2813-2817 — _col_letter_dp handles A-Z + AA-AZ (up to col 51). Partial but good enough for its own use. Not touching.

 Fix: add a local _col_letter(idx) helper to each of the 3 scripts (same shape as signal_processor._col_letter ) and replace the bare chr(65 + col) / chr(ord("A") + idx) calls. Not importing across module

 boundaries — these are one-shot audit scripts and a 6-line helper is simpler than a cross-module dependency. Out of scope: scripts/phase1d_local_sentinel.py:86 (not flagged by the audit, debug print).

 116 )

 119 + def _col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 120 + """Convert 0-indexed column number to A1 letter (A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...).

 121 +

 122 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(65 + col)` which wraps past Z

 123 + silently — chr ( 65 + 26 ) = '[' , breaking any write to a column at index ≥ 26.

 124 + """

 125 + if idx < 0 :

 126 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 127 + result = ""

 128 + while True :

 129 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 130 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 131 + if idx < 0 :

 132 + break

 133 + return result

 134 +

 135 +

 62 def col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 63 - # 0-indexed → A, B, C...

 64 - return chr(ord("A") + idx)

 63 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 65 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): previous code was `chr(ord("A") + idx)` which

 66 + wraps past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Fixed to handle overflow.

 67 + """

 68 + if idx < 0 :

 69 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 70 + result = ""

 71 + while True :

 72 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 73 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 74 + if idx < 0 :

 75 + break

 76 + return result

 78 +

 79 def dump_row ( abs_row_num: int , row: list ):

 80 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 81 print ( f"\n━━━ Row {abs_row_num} (len= { len (row)} ) ━━━" )

 28 + def _col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 29 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 30 +

 31 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(ord('A') + i)` which wraps

 32 + past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Currently only called with i in

 33 + range ( 20 ) so the wrap cannot manifest, but this is a defensive

 34 + upgrade that matches the fix in enrich_c4_pass2.py and

 35 + phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py.

 36 + """

 37 + if idx < 0 :

 38 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 39 + result = ""

 40 + while True :

 41 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 42 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 43 + if idx < 0 :

 44 + break

 45 + return result

 46 +

 47 +

 48 def filled_tup ( row ):

 49 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 50 return tuple (i for i, v in enumerate (padded[: 20 ]) if str (v).strip() != "" )

 11 commits , all pushed. ctx marker reads 30% / 20% to EOS (measured). We're at 10 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) below the measured 40-percent pause threshold — still under, so per your directive I
 keep going.

 1. Theme #4 — scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — pure error-message polish across many files. Safe, mechanical.

 2. Theme #5 — broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters — case-by-case, highest cognitive load.

 3. Theme #6 — hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — judgement calls, not mechanical.

 4. HIGH-5 gas/Code.gs:189 create Draft Reply empty body bug — one-line fix but needs another GAS redeploy walkthrough with you.

 My recommendation: Theme #4 (.env missing) next. It's the safest remaining mechanical theme — zero runtime behavior change on the happy path, just better error messages when someone runs a script without the
 env loaded. Each file gets a load_dotenv + env-var check + SystemExit with a helpful message. Low ctx cost per file, wide blast radius.

 10 commits, all pushed, Railway auto-redeployed. ctx marker reads 26% / 24% to EOS (measured) — still below the measured 40-percent pause threshold.

 Next theme options from the audit (priority order):

 1. chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic — 3 files ( enrich_c4_pass2.py , phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py , phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py ). Wraps past column Z silently. Small mechanical fix.

 2. scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — many files, pure error-message polish.

 3. Hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — needs judgement calls per site, less mechanical.

 4. Broad except Exception: pass — case-by-case review, highest cognitive load, lowest automation.

 My recommendation: Theme #3 = chr(65+col) column arithmetic. It's the highest-leverage remaining mechanical theme, the 3 scripts are one-shot audit helpers so blast radius is contained, and the fix is a tiny

 shared helper (converts 0-indexed column to A/B/…/Z/AA/AB/… letter). After that, ctx budget permitting, Theme #4 (.env missing) is pure polish.

 ※ recap: Draining the S70 overnight audit HIGHs and themes; 10 commits pushed to main, currently queued on Theme #3 (chr(65+col) column-letter arithmetic in 3 scripts). Next action: start Theme #3 unless you

 redirect.

 ❯ lets go theme 3

 ⏺ Theme #3 — chr(65+col) fix. Let me find the sites and check if a correct helper already exists in the codebase so we can reuse instead of re-inventing.

 ⏺ Theme #3 plan

 Buggy sites (measured):

 - scripts/enrich_c4_pass2.py — 4 sheet-write sites at lines 972, 980, 1009, 1020. Load-bearing (writes wrong cells if column index ≥ 26). Latent today because state_col=0 , parent_col=6 , notes_col=18 — all

 under Z — but fragile to future column additions.

 - scripts/phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py:62-64 — local col_letter() helper. Used in a debug print at line 73. Not load-bearing but fixing for consistency.

 - scripts/phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py:48 — debug print inside range(20) loop. Cannot manifest today (20 < 26) but fixing for consistency with the audit callout.

 Already-correct helpers:

 - tools/signal_processor.py:258-266 — canonical _col_letter with proper base-26 loop. ✅

 - tools/district_prospector.py:2813-2817 — _col_letter_dp handles A-Z + AA-AZ (up to col 51). Partial but good enough for its own use. Not touching.

 Fix: add a local _col_letter(idx) helper to each of the 3 scripts (same shape as signal_processor._col_letter ) and replace the bare chr(65 + col) / chr(ord("A") + idx) calls. Not importing across module

 boundaries — these are one-shot audit scripts and a 6-line helper is simpler than a cross-module dependency. Out of scope: scripts/phase1d_local_sentinel.py:86 (not flagged by the audit, debug print).

 ⏺ Update ( scripts/enrich_c4_pass2.py )

 ⎿   Added 17 lines

 116 )

 119 + def _col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 120 + """Convert 0-indexed column number to A1 letter (A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...).

 121 +

 122 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(65 + col)` which wraps past Z

 123 + silently — chr ( 65 + 26 ) = '[' , breaking any write to a column at index ≥ 26.

 124 + """

 125 + if idx < 0 :

 126 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 127 + result = ""

 128 + while True :

 129 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 130 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 131 + if idx < 0 :

 132 + break

 133 + return result

 134 +

 135 +

 137 """Try to extract US state from email domain. Returns state abbrev or None."""

 138 if not email or "@" not in email:

 ⏺ Update ( scripts/phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py )

 ⎿   Added 14 lines, removed 2 lines

 62 def col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 63 - # 0-indexed → A, B, C...

 64 - return chr(ord("A") + idx)

 63 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 65 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): previous code was `chr(ord("A") + idx)` which

 66 + wraps past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Fixed to handle overflow.

 67 + """

 68 + if idx < 0 :

 69 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 70 + result = ""

 71 + while True :

 72 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 73 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 74 + if idx < 0 :

 75 + break

 76 + return result

 78 +

 79 def dump_row ( abs_row_num: int , row: list ):

 80 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 81 print ( f"\n━━━ Row {abs_row_num} (len= { len (row)} ) ━━━" )

 25 ).execute()

 28 + def _col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 29 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 30 +

 31 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(ord('A') + i)` which wraps

 32 + past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Currently only called with i in

 33 + range ( 20 ) so the wrap cannot manifest, but this is a defensive

 34 + upgrade that matches the fix in enrich_c4_pass2.py and

 35 + phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py.

 36 + """

 37 + if idx < 0 :

 38 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 39 + result = ""

 40 + while True :

 41 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 42 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 43 + if idx < 0 :

 44 + break

 45 + return result

 46 +

 47 +

 48 def filled_tup ( row ):

 49 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 50 return tuple (i for i, v in enumerate (padded[: 20 ]) if str (v).strip() != "" )

 116 )

 119 + def _col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 120 + """Convert 0-indexed column number to A1 letter (A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...).

 121 +

 122 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(65 + col)` which wraps past Z

 123 + silently — chr ( 65 + 26 ) = '[' , breaking any write to a column at index ≥ 26.

 124 + """

 125 + if idx < 0 :

 126 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 127 + result = ""

 128 + while True :

 129 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 130 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 131 + if idx < 0 :

 132 + break

 133 + return result

 62 def col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 63 - # 0-indexed → A, B, C...

 63 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 65 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): previous code was `chr(ord("A") + idx)` which

 66 + wraps past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Fixed to handle overflow.

 67 + """

 68 + if idx < 0 :

 69 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 70 + result = ""

 72 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 73 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 74 + if idx < 0 :

 75 + break

 76 + return result

 62 def col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 63 - # 0-indexed → A, B, C...

 64 - return chr(ord("A") + idx)

 63 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 65 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): previous code was `chr(ord("A") + idx)` which

 66 + wraps past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Fixed to handle overflow.

 67 + """

 68 + if idx < 0 :

 69 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 70 + result = ""

 71 + while True :

 72 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 73 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 74 + if idx < 0 :

 75 + break

 76 + return result

 78 +

 79 def dump_row ( abs_row_num: int , row: list ):

 80 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 81 print ( f"\n━━━ Row {abs_row_num} (len= { len (row)} ) ━━━" )

 29 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 30 +

 31 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): replaces bare `chr(ord('A') + i)` which wraps

 32 + past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Currently only called with i in

 33 + range ( 20 ) so the wrap cannot manifest, but this is a defensive

 34 + upgrade that matches the fix in enrich_c4_pass2.py and

 35 + phase1_ground_truth_lackland.py.

 36 + """

 37 + if idx < 0 :

 38 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 39 + result = ""

 40 + while True :

 41 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 42 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 43 + if idx < 0 :

 44 + break

 45 + return result

 46 +

 47 +

 48 def filled_tup ( row ):

 49 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 50 return tuple (i for i, v in enumerate (padded[: 20 ]) if str (v).strip() != "" )

 11 commits , all pushed. ctx marker reads 30% / 20% to EOS (measured). We're at 10 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) below the measured 40-percent pause threshold — still under, so per your directive I

 keep going.

 1. Theme #4 — scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — pure error-message polish across many files. Safe, mechanical.

 2. Theme #5 — broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters — case-by-case, highest cognitive load.

 3. Theme #6 — hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — judgement calls, not mechanical.

 4. HIGH-5 gas/Code.gs:189 create Draft Reply empty body bug — one-line fix but needs another GAS redeploy walkthrough with you.

 My recommendation: Theme #4 (.env missing) next. It's the safest remaining mechanical theme — zero runtime behavior change on the happy path, just better error messages when someone runs a script without the

 env loaded. Each file gets a load_dotenv + env-var check + SystemExit with a helpful message. Low ctx cost per file, wide blast radius.

 Alternatively if you'd rather do HIGH-5 now and I can walk you through the GAS redeploy again (same flow as this morning), that drains the last HIGH and moves HIGH-5 off the parked list.

 What do you want — Theme #4 (.env), HIGH-5 (GAS redeploy), or something else?

 ※ recap: Draining the S70 audit: 11 commits pushed (CRIT-2 verified, 7 HIGHs, 3 themes). Awaiting your pick for the next theme — .env-missing polish, HIGH-5 GAS redeploy, or something else.

 ❯ Let's go with your recommendation

 ⏺ Theme #4 — .env crash polish. Scoping first so I know how big this is.

 ⏺ Theme #4 scope. 31 script files reference .env , 12 directly access os.environ["KEY"] . Fixing every file is too broad for one commit. Better strategy: create a shared helper, then update the currently-active

 scripts to use it . Pattern gets established for future scripts; dead one-shots from past bug investigations stay untouched.

 First, checking the existing .env loading pattern and confirming imports work between scripts.

 ⏺ Current patterns in 3 active scripts:

 - load_campaign.py:68-73 — load_dotenv() wrapped in try/except: pass — swallows all errors, downstream KeyError on missing vars.

 - f4_serper_replay.py:41 — manual Path.read_text() — raw File Not Found Error on missing .env .

 - fetch_csta_roster.py:44 — same manual pattern, same raw crash.

 All three patterns fail without telling the user what's wrong or what to do. Building the shared helper first.

 62 def col_letter ( idx: int ) -> str :

 63 - # 0-indexed → A, B, C...

 64 - return chr(ord("A") + idx)

 63 + """0-indexed → A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ... — base-26 with carry.

 65 + HIGH theme #3 (S70): previous code was `chr(ord("A") + idx)` which

 66 + wraps past Z silently ( chr ( 65 + 26 )= '[' ). Fixed to handle overflow.

 67 + """

 68 + if idx < 0 :

 69 + raise ValueError( f"negative column index: {idx} " )

 70 + result = ""

 71 + while True :

 72 + result = chr ( 65 + idx % 26 ) + result

 73 + idx = idx // 26 - 1

 74 + if idx < 0 :

 75 + break

 76 + return result

 78 +

 79 def dump_row ( abs_row_num: int , row: list ):

 80 padded = row + [ "" ] * max ( 0 , 20 - len (row))

 81 print ( f"\n━━━ Row {abs_row_num} (len= { len (row)} ) ━━━" )

 - fetch_csta_roster.py:44 — same manual pattern, same raw crash.

 ⏺ Theme #4 — .env crash polish. Scoping first so I know how big this is.

 ⏺ Theme #4 scope. 31 script files reference .env , 12 directly access os.environ["KEY"] . Fixing every file is too broad for one commit. Better strategy: create a shared helper, then update the currently-active

 scripts to use it . Pattern gets established for future scripts; dead one-shots from past bug investigations stay untouched.

 First, checking the existing .env loading pattern and confirming imports work between scripts.

 ⏺ Current patterns in 3 active scripts:

 - load_campaign.py:68-73 — load_dotenv() wrapped in try/except: pass — swallows all errors, downstream KeyError on missing vars.

 - f4_serper_replay.py:41 — manual Path.read_text() — raw File Not Found Error on missing .env .

 - fetch_csta_roster.py:44 — same manual pattern, same raw crash.

 All three patterns fail without telling the user what's wrong or what to do. Building the shared helper first.

 11 commits , all pushed. ctx marker reads 30% / 20% to EOS (measured). We're at 10 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) below the measured 40-percent pause threshold — still under, so per your directive I

 keep going.

 1. Theme #4 — scripts/*.py crash on missing .env — pure error-message polish across many files. Safe, mechanical.

 2. Theme #5 — broad except Exception: pass swallowing counters — case-by-case, highest cognitive load.

 3. Theme #6 — hardcoded timestamp windows ( "2026-04-" , DRIP_DAYS ) — judgement calls, not mechanical.

 4. HIGH-5 gas/Code.gs:189 create Draft Reply empty body bug — one-line fix but needs another GAS redeploy walkthrough with you.

 My recommendation: Theme #4 (.env missing) next. It's the safest remaining mechanical theme — zero runtime behavior change on the happy path, just better error messages when someone runs a script without the

 env loaded. Each file gets a load_dotenv + env-var check + SystemExit with a helpful message. Low ctx cost per file, wide blast radius.

 Alternatively if you'd rather do HIGH-5 now and I can walk you through the GAS redeploy again (same flow as this morning), that drains the last HIGH and moves HIGH-5 off the parked list.

 What do you want — Theme #4 (.env), HIGH-5 (GAS redeploy), or something else?

 Gallopi

 ⏺ Theme #4 scope. 31 script files reference .env , 12 directly access os.environ["KEY"] . Fixing every file is too broad for one commit. Better strategy: create a shared helper, then update the currently-active

 scripts to use it . Pattern gets established for future scripts; dead one-shots from past bug investigations stay untouched.

 First, checking the existing .env loading pattern and confirming imports work between scripts.

 ⏺ Current patterns in 3 active scripts:

 - load_campaign.py:68-73 — load_dotenv() wrapped in try/except: pass — swallows all errors, downstream KeyError on missing vars.

 - f4_serper_replay.py:41 — manual Path.read_text() — raw File Not Found Error on missing .env .

 - fetch_csta_roster.py:44 — same manual pattern, same raw crash.

 All three patterns fail without telling the user what's wrong or what to do. Building the shared helper first.

 7 load_env_or_die(required=[ "SERPER_API_KEY" , "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" ])

 9 On failure (missing file, parse error, missing required var) this raises

 10 SystemExit with a human-readable message naming the exact problem. Never

 Fixing the load_campaign.py required-vars list. OUTREACH_REFRESH_TOKEN is not stored in .env (Scout fetches it dynamically via the OAuth flow using CLIENT_ID + CLIENT_SECRET + REDIRECT_URI), so requiring it
 would block a script that previously worked.

 ⏺ Update ( scripts/load_campaign.py )

 ⎿   Added 6 lines, removed 1 line
 70 # shared helper so missing .env / missing vars fail loudly with a useful
 71 # message instead of a downstream KeyError.
 72 from _env import load_env_or_die # noqa: E402
 73 + # OUTREACH_REFRESH_TOKEN intentionally NOT required here — Scout fetches
 74 + # the refresh token dynamically via OAuth using CLIENT_ID + CLIENT_SECRET
 75 + # + REDIRECT_URI, so the token is not kept in .env. If it's missing at
 76 + # runtime, tools.outreach_client surfaces a clear error on the first API

 77 + # call; we don't need to duplicate that check here.

 79 "OUTREACH_CLIENT_ID" ,

 80 "OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET" ,

 81 + "OUTREACH_REDIRECT_URI" ,

 Fixing the load_campaign.py required-vars list. OUTREACH_REFRESH_TOKEN is not stored in .env (Scout fetches it dynamically via the OAuth flow using CLIENT_ID + CLIENT_SECRET + REDIRECT_URI), so requiring it

 would block a script that previously worked.

 70 # shared helper so missing .env / missing vars fail loudly with a useful

 71 # message instead of a downstream KeyError.

 72 from _env import load_env_or_die # noqa: E402

 73 + # OUTREACH_REFRESH_TOKEN intentionally NOT required here — Scout fetches

 74 + # the refresh token dynamically via OAuth using CLIENT_ID + CLIENT_SECRET

 75 + # + REDIRECT_URI, so the token is not kept in .env. If it's missing at

 76 + # runtime, tools.outreach_client surfaces a clear error on the first API

 77 + # call; we don't need to duplicate that check here.

 78 load_env_or_die(required=[

 79 "OUTREACH_CLIENT_ID" ,

 80 "OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET" ,

 76 - "OUTREACH_REFRESH_TOKEN",

 81 + "OUTREACH_REDIRECT_URI" ,

 12 commits all pushed. ctx marker reads 33% / 17% to EOS (measured). 7 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) from the measured 40-percent pause threshold — getting close to your stopping line.

 Session incident log from this turn

 Two things went wrong during Theme #4 verification that you should know about:

 1. Several scripts don't have argparse guards. When I ran python scripts/foo.py --help as a smoke test, audit_c4_prospects.py , enrich_c4_pass2.py , and fetch_csta_roster.py ignored --help and started executing
 their full top-level bodies. I caught enrich_c4_pass2.py mid-read and killed it at line 415 ( C4 prospects: 1274 ) — the write site at line 1056 was never reached, so zero sheet writes happened from that one.
 audit_c4_prospects.py is read-only, so it just printed row counts and exited cleanly.

 2. fetch_csta_roster.py accidentally wrote to memory/csta_roster.json during that same --help test — it re-ran the real fetch, updated fetched_at from 2026-04-14 to 2026-04-15 , and dropped the "+ S63
 hand-curation (IN, TN)" suffix from the source field. The IN/TN hand-curated entries may have been overwritten with auto-extracted data. I reverted memory/csta_roster.json via git checkout HEAD -- before
 committing Theme #4 , so the S63 hand-curation is preserved. Working-tree change was the only damage.

 Lesson documented in the Theme #4 commit message: scripts that do work at top-level (no if __name__ == "__main__" guard) should not be smoke-tested with --help . A proper cleanup would add argparse guards to
 those 3 scripts, but that's separate scope.

 ---

 ctx 33% measured / 17% to EOS measured. We're 7 percentage points (measured) from your measured 40-percent pause threshold. The next theme (either HIGH-5 with a GAS redeploy, Theme #5 except: pass
 case-by-case, or Theme #6 hardcoded timestamps) could push us over that line depending on scope.

 ctx 33% measured / 17% to EOS measured. We're 7 percentage points (measured) from your measured 40-percent pause threshold. The next theme (either HIGH-5 with a GAS redeploy, Theme #5 except: pass
 ase-by-case, or Theme #6 hardcoded timestamps) could push us over that line depending on cope.

 Recommendation: pause here, wait for the next ctx: tick to confirm headroom, and ask what you want. Options:

 - (a) Keep going with the next theme. Theme #5 ( except: pass ) needs judgement calls per site and isn't mechanical — I'd scope it first before committing. HIGH-5 is a 2-line code fix but needs another GAS
 redeploy walkthrough (interactive).

 - (b) Pause for EOS wrap-up. 12 commits is a strong S70 haul; this is a clean stopping point. I can prep the EOS handoff now.

 - (c) Something else — name it.

 Which?

 ❯ pause for wrap up . We're approaching context limits. Before I hit /exit, do the following in order:

 1. Save any feedback, preferences, or user context learned this session to memory

 2. Update the project plan file (e.g. SCOUT_PLAN.md):

 - Mark completed items as done

 - Add any new tasks or additions that came up this session

 - Update 'Current Focus' to reflect exactly where we are

 - If we're mid-task, note precisely what was being attempted and what state it's in

 3. Maintain CLAUDE.md:

 - Add any new rules, conventions, or architectural decisions from this session

 - Update 'Current State' with: what's working, what's broken or in-progress, and the exact next step to take

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 12 commits all pushed. ctx marker reads 33% / 17% to EOS (measured). 7 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) from the measured 40-percent pause threshold — getting close to your stopping line.

 Session incident log from this turn

 Two things went wrong during Theme #4 verification that you should know about:

 1. Several scripts don't have argparse guards. When I ran python scripts/foo.py --help as a smoke test, audit_c4_prospects.py , enrich_c4_pass2.py , and fetch_csta_roster.py ignored --help and started executing

 their full top-level bodies. I caught enrich_c4_pass2.py mid-read and killed it at line 415 ( C4 prospects: 1274 ) — the write site at line 1056 was never reached, so zero sheet writes happened from that one.

 audit_c4_prospects.py is read-only, so it just printed row counts and exited cleanly.

 2. fetch_csta_roster.py accidentally wrote to memory/csta_roster.json during that same --help test — it re-ran the real fetch, updated fetched_at from 2026-04-14 to 2026-04-15 , and dropped the "+ S63

 hand-curation (IN, TN)" suffix from the source field. The IN/TN hand-curated entries may have been overwritten with auto-extracted data. I reverted memory/csta_roster.json via git checkout HEAD -- before

 committing Theme #4 , so the S63 hand-curation is preserved. Working-tree change was the only damage.

 Lesson documented in the Theme #4 commit message: scripts that do work at top-level (no if __name__ == "__main__" guard) should not be smoke-tested with --help . A proper cleanup would add argparse guards to

 those 3 scripts, but that's separate scope.

 ---

 ctx 33% measured / 17% to EOS measured. We're 7 percentage points (measured) from your measured 40-percent pause threshold. The next theme (either HIGH-5 with a GAS redeploy, Theme #5 except: pass

 case-by-case, or Theme #6 hardcoded timestamps) could push us over that line depending on scope.

 Recommendation: pause here, wait for the next ctx: tick to confirm headroom, and ask what you want. Options:

 - (a) Keep going with the next theme. Theme #5 ( except: pass ) needs judgement calls per site and isn't mechanical — I'd scope it first before committing. HIGH-5 is a 2-line code fix but needs another GAS

 redeploy walkthrough (interactive).

 - (b) Pause for EOS wrap-up. 12 commits is a strong S70 haul; this is a clean stopping point. I can prep the EOS handoff now.

 - (c) Something else — name it.

 Which?

 ※ recap: Draining the overnight audit backlog: 12 commits shipped (2 CRITs, 7 HIGHs, 4 themes), all pushed to main. Next action: pick between keeping going (HIGH-5 or Theme #5), or wrapping to EOS since ctx is

 33% measured.

 ❯ pause for wrap up . We're approaching context limits. Before I hit /exit, do the following in order:

 1. Save any feedback, preferences, or user context learned this session to memory

 2. Update the project plan file (e.g. SCOUT_PLAN.md):

 - Mark completed items as done

 - Add any new tasks or additions that came up this session

 - Update 'Current Focus' to reflect exactly where we are

 - If we're mid-task, note precisely what was being attempted and what state it's in

 3. Maintain CLAUDE.md:

 - Add any new rules, conventions, or architectural decisions from this session

 - Update 'Current State' with: what's working, what's broken or in-progress, and the exact next step to take

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 12 commits all pushed. ctx marker reads 33% / 17% to EOS (measured). 7 percentage points (measured, arithmetic) from the measured 40-percent pause threshold — getting close to your stopping line.

 Session incident log from this turn

 Two things went wrong during Theme #4 verification that you should know about:

 1. Several scripts don't have argparse guards. When I ran python scripts/foo.py --help as a smoke test, audit_c4_prospects.py , enrich_c4_pass2.py , and fetch_csta_roster.py ignored --help and started executing

 their full top-level bodies. I caught enrich_c4_pass2.py mid-read and killed it at line 415 ( C4 prospects: 1274 ) — the write site at line 1056 was never reached, so zero sheet writes happened from that one.

 audit_c4_prospects.py is read-only, so it just printed row counts and exited cleanly.

 2. fetch_csta_roster.py accidentally wrote to memory/csta_roster.json during that same --help test — it re-ran the real fetch, updated fetched_at from 2026-04-14 to 2026-04-15 , and dropped the "+ S63

 hand-curation (IN, TN)" suffix from the source field. The IN/TN hand-curated entries may have been overwritten with auto-extracted data. I reverted memory/csta_roster.json via git checkout HEAD -- before

 committing Theme #4 , so the S63 hand-curation is preserved. Working-tree change was the only damage.

 Lesson documented in the Theme #4 commit message: scripts that do work at top-level (no if __name__ == "__main__" guard) should not be smoke-tested with --help . A proper cleanup would add argparse guards to

 those 3 scripts, but that's separate scope.

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 16 + - **Remaining audit work (parked for S71+):** HIGH-5 (GAS redeploy bundle), Theme #5 (`except: pass` per-site review, not mechanical), Theme #6 (hardcoded timestamp windows, per-site judgement),

 17 + - **S67 sequence-build focus STILL parked.** S67 was supposed to be "build email sequences for the 23 active prospecting strategies" but S68/S69/S70 spent their cycles on the overnight audit dra

 + in instead. **S71 first action: confirm Thursday 2026-04-16 diocesan drip state** (if date has passed without `scripts/diocesan_drip.py --execute` running, contact Steven before any other work).
